Hyndburn Transparency Scorecard: How Open Is Your Council?

Key Findings

  • Hyndburn Borough Council’s spending data covers £211.3M across 29,804 transactions from 2016/17 to 2025/26.
  • The council engaged with 2,395 unique suppliers over the ten-year period.
  • Overall spending increased by £2.2M, representing a 29.7% rise from 2017 to 2026.
  • A significant spending increase of £71.7M (1587.9%) occurred between 2023 and 2024.
  • Spending is projected to decrease by £61,466,496 (-86.6%) from 2025 to 2026.

Hyndburn's Spending Landscape

Hyndburn Borough Council has published spending data totalling £211.3M across 29,804 individual transactions. This comprehensive dataset spans ten financial years, from 2016/17 to 2025/26. The information provides a detailed look into how public money is allocated and spent within the district. Over this period, the council has worked with a substantial number of organisations, engaging 2,395 unique suppliers.

Analysing the spending over the entire period reveals a notable increase. From 2017 to 2026, Hyndburn Borough Council’s annual spending increased by £2.2M. This represents an overall percentage rise of 29.7%. These figures highlight the dynamic financial environment within which the council operates, reflecting changes in service provision, infrastructure projects, and operational costs over a decade.

Analysing Spending Trends

A closer examination of Hyndburn Borough Council’s year-on-year spending reveals significant fluctuations. The data shows periods of both growth and contraction in expenditure. For instance, spending rose by +£1.9M (+26.2%) from 2017 to 2018, reaching £9.2M. This was followed by a substantial decrease of £-2,834,079 (-30.7%) in 2019, bringing the total down to £6.4M. Such variations can indicate shifts in council priorities or responses to external economic factors.

The most striking change in spending occurred between 2023 and 2024. The council’s expenditure surged by an extraordinary £71.7M, marking an increase of 1587.9%. This pushed the annual spend from £4.5M in 2023 to £76.2M in 2024. Following this peak, spending is projected to decrease significantly. From 2024 to 2025, a reduction of £-5,256,237 (-6.9%) is shown, bringing the figure to £70.9M. A further projected decrease of £-61,466,496 (-86.6%) is indicated for 2026, with spending returning to £9.5M. These dramatic shifts warrant public attention and further inquiry to understand the underlying causes and impacts on local services.

The table below provides a detailed breakdown of Hyndburn Borough Council's annual spending and the year-on-year changes, illustrating the scale of these financial movements. Consistent publication of this data allows residents to track how public funds are managed and to identify periods of unusual expenditure or savings. Understanding these patterns is crucial for informed civic engagement and accountability.

Financial Year Annual Spend Year-on-Year Change Percentage Change
2017 £7.3M
2018 £9.2M +£1.9M +26.2%
2019 £6.4M £-2,834,079 -30.7%
2020 £8.0M +£1.6M +25.3%
2021 £9.5M +£1.4M +18.0%
2022 £9.8M +£315K +3.3%
2023 £4.5M £-5,249,992 -53.8%
2024 £76.2M +£71.7M +1587.9%
2025 £70.9M £-5,256,237 -6.9%
2026 £9.5M £-61,466,496 -86.6%

Data Coverage and Transparency

Hyndburn Borough Council's commitment to transparency is demonstrated through its publication of 29,804 transactions over a ten-year span. This extensive data coverage, from 2016/17 to 2025/26, provides a valuable resource for residents. It allows for detailed scrutiny of financial decisions and helps to build trust between the council and the community it serves. The sheer volume of data, alongside the engagement with 2,395 unique suppliers, paints a comprehensive picture of the council's operational reach.

Consistent and detailed data publication is a cornerstone of local government transparency. It enables residents to understand how public funds are utilised, to question significant spending variations, and to hold elected officials accountable. The availability of this data on platforms like AI DOGE supports informed public discourse about council finances and performance. It empowers citizens to become active participants in local governance.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Hyndburn Borough Council about the specific reasons for the significant spending fluctuations, particularly the increase of £71.7M in 2024 and the projected decrease of £61,466,496 in 2026.
  • Attend council committee meetings, as they are open to the public, to hear discussions about financial planning and budget allocations.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to understand the nature of major agreements and the suppliers involved in large expenditures.
  • Write to your local councillor about the council's financial planning, large spending variations, and how these impact local services.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data for Hyndburn Borough Council and other Lancashire councils yourself.
This article is based on Hyndburn Borough Council's published spending data covering 2016/17 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
